This section describes how to generate a technology library, and the various inputs and outputs of this flow.
Required Input Files

- technology LEF
- cell LEF
- Quantus technology file
- LEFDEF layermap - optional. If you do not specify this file, Voltus will automatically generate it.
Procedure
To create a technology power-grid view, complete the following steps:

1. Read the LEF files for all the cells to be characterized. 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 read_lib -lef ../data/lef/tech.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/cell_macro.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/pso_header.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/pso_ring.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/pll.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/decap.lef
2. Specify the settings for technology library characterization through the command line or through the GUI. 
 GUI : Select Power & Rail - Set PowerGrid Library Mode . 
 In the GUI, the fields in the Advanced tab are disabled for the Tech Only cell type, except for the Power Gate Cell Characterization field. When generating technology library, if power gating cells are specified, the ron, idsat, and ileak parameters need to be specified as well. Power gating cells can be characterized using Spice netlist and model when generating standard cell power-grid library.
 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 set_pg_library_mode 
 
 -extraction_tech_file RCgen.tch 
 
 -lef_layermap lefdef.map 
 
 -celltype techonly 
 
 -power_pins { VDD 1.08 VDDO 1.08 VDDG 1.08 } 
 
 -ground_pins { VSS GND VSSG } 
 
 -temperature -40 
 
 -powergate_parameters { { cell1 VDDG VDD 100 0.003 0.00015 } { cell2 VDDG VDD 100 0.003 0.00015 } }
3. Generate the output for power-grid library generation through the command line or through the GUI. 
 GUI : Select Power & Rail - Generate PowerGrid Library . 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 generate_pg_library -output tech_pgv
The log file will generate the power-grid view generation statistics, as shown in the following log file snippet:
Output
The output directory will contain the following information:

data:
The default name of the technology library is techonly.cl . Tech views are LEF pin-based views. Included inside tech views: 
 
 User specified area based cell capacitance is distributed on power-pins evenly 
 Current taps (sinks) are added to the power-pins 
 Total current for the cell is distributed evenly between multiple current taps on each rail. 
 No visibility of current distribution and power-grid inside cell

A report for each cell's power/ground net containing: 
 
 name of power and ground net, voltage value for power pins 
 capacitance value which is area-based 
 number of taps 
 user-defined Idsat, Ileakage, and Ron for POWERGATE cell

report containing: 
 
 cell types: STDCELL, FILLER, DECOUPLING CAP, POWER_GATE 
 pins, metal layers, number of current taps, etc.

lines: For standard cells, Voltus generates all three views: Early, EM, and IR, which are identical to each other. These views for standard cell libraries can improve the accuracy of the static and dynamic IRdrop and EM analysis. Voltus uses SPICE subcircuit and Spectre simulation to generate decoupled capacitance for standard cells. For power-gates, it characterizes Ron, Idsat and Ileakage.
This section describes how to generate a standard cell power-grid library, and the various inputs and outputs of this flow.
Required Input Files

- technology LEF
- cell LEF
- Quantus technology file
- LEFDEF layermap - optional. If you do not specify this file, Voltus will automatically generate it.
- Spice netlists for standard cells
- Spectre file that points to the Spectre models, or Spice models
Procedure
To create power-grid views for standard cells, complete the following steps:

1. Read the LEF files for all the cells to be characterized. 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 read_lib -lef ../data/lef/tech.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/buf_ao.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/pso_header.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/pso_ring.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/decap.lef
2. Specify the settings for standard cell library characterization through the command line or through the GUI. 
 GUI : Select Power & Rail - Set PowerGrid Library Mode . 
 You can perform standard cell PGV generation without the extraction technology file (qrcTechFile) and LEF layermap file. However, the LEF layer names should be consistent in all PGVs. When you merge a PGV generated without the technology file, with a PGV generated with the technology file and lefdef layermap files, the layermap would be automatically corrected in the merged PGV. 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 set_pg_library_mode \ 
 
 -ground_pins VSS \ 
 
 -power_pins {VDD 0.9 TVDD 0.9} \ 
 
 -decap_cells {DECAP8 DECAP64 DECAP4 DECAP32 DECAP2 DECAP16 DECAP1}\ 
 
 -filler_cells { FILL8 FILL64 FILL4 FILL32 FILL2 FILL16 FILL1}\ 
 
 -celltype stdcells \ 
 
 -cell_decap_file ../data/voltus/decap.cmd \ 
 
 -cell_list_file ../data/voltus/cell.list \ 
 
 -spice_subckts { \ 
 
 ../data/netlists/gsclib090.sp \ 
 
 ../data/netlists/pso_header.spi \ 
 
 ../data/netlists/pso_ring.spi } \ 
 
 - lef_layermap ../data/voltus/lefdef.layermap \ 
 
 -current_distribution propagation \ 
 
 -spice_models ../data/netlists/spectre_load.sp \ 
 
 -extraction_tech_file ../data/qrc/gpdk090_9l.tch \ 
 
 - powergate_parameters { \ 
 
 {RING_SWITCH TVDD VDD} \ 
 
 {HEADER_SWITCH TVDD VDD} }
3. Generate the output for power-grid library generation through the command line or through the GUI. 
 GUI : Select Power & Rail - Generate PowerGrid Library . 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 generate_pg_library -output stdcell_pgv
The log file will generate the power-grid view generation statistics, as shown in the following log file snippet:
Output
The output directory will contain the following information:

data:
The default name of the standard cell library is stdcells .cl . Included inside standard cells power-grid views: 
 
 Accurate coupling capacitance between power and ground pins is calculated from Spice simulation. 
 "Current Taps" are added to the power-pins.

A report for each cell's power/ground net containing: 
 
 name of power and ground nets, voltage value for power pins 
 capacitance value which is simulation based 
 number of taps 
 Idsat, Ileakage and Ron for power gate cells calculated based on simulation 
 detailed information for power gate cells

report containing: 
 
 cell types: STDCELL, FILLER, DECOUPLING CAP, POWER_GATE 
 pins, metal layers, number of current taps, etc.

title: Creating a Power-grid View for a Macro Cell

lines: Macro power-grid views are generated to accurately capture current and capacitance distribution inside the memory, macro, and IO cells. For macro/memory/IO, Voltus generates all three views: Early, EM, and IR.

The library generation requires GDS and spice subckt with device X-Y location. The library characterizer simulates the spice subckt and based on spice device models it calculates accurate capacitance and current distribution for the cell. The power net parasitics are extracted down to contact or up to the user-specified via layer, and current taps are snapped to the nearest via location.

If you have a spice netlist without device X-Y location, the accurate views will still capture the correct capacitance but it will distribute the total current evenly on the current taps. This could potentially mask the real IRdrop and EM problem inside the cell or result in false violations.
This section describes how to generate a macro cell power-grid library, and the various inputs and outputs of this flow.
Required Input Files

- technology LEF
- cell LEF
- Quantus technology file
- LEFDEF layermap - optional. If you do not specify this file, Voltus will automatically generate it.
- Spice netlist for the macro cell
- Spectre file that points to the Spectre models, or Spice models
- GDS file of the macro
- GDS layermap 
 
For macro PGV generation, the input files should contain only one macro definition. That is, each LEF, netlist (Spice or CDL), and GDS file should contain only one macro definition.
Procedure
To create power-grid views for a macro cell, complete the following steps:

1. Read the LEF file for the cell to be characterized. 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 read_lib -lef ../data/lef/tech.lef \ 
 
 ../data/lef/pll.lef
2. Specify the settings for macro cell library characterization through the command line or through the GUI. 
 GUI : Select Power & Rail - Set PowerGrid Library Mode . 
 In the in the Advanced tab, you can specify the following: 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 set_pg_library_mode \ 
 
 -gds_files ../data/gds/pll.gds \ 
 
 -ground_pins VSS \ 
 
 -cell_list_file ../data/voltus/macro.list \ 
 
 -power_pins {VDD 0.9} \ 
 
 -celltype macros \ 
 
 -spice_subckts ../data/netlists/pll.sp \ 
 
 -gds_layermap ../data/voltus/gds.layermap \ 
 
 - lef_layermap ../data/voltus/lefdef.layermap \ 
 
 -stop@via CONT \ 
 
 -spice_models ../data/netlists/spectre_load.sp \ 
 
 -current_distribution propagation \ 
 
 -extraction_tech_file ../data/qrc/gpdk090_9l.tch
  - Stop@via: Specifies to stop the extraction of the power-grid network at the specified via layer. VIA_1 is the commonly used layer to create current tap. CONT diffusion contact layer is used when IP is not qualified for EM. Upper layers, such as VIA_2 and VIA_3 , are used when IP is qualified for EM and its effect is to be seen at the top level. Higher layer reduces the memory, disk footprint, and runtime due to reduced resistor and node count.
  - Current Distribution : Specifies the method of current distribution inside the cell.
3. Generate the output for power-grid library generation through the command line or through the GUI. 
 GUI : Select Power & Rail - Generate PowerGrid Library . 
 Tcl command : 
 
 
 
 generate_pg_library -output macro_pgv
The log file will generate the power-grid view generation statistics, as shown in the following log file snippet:
Output
The output directory will contain the following information:

data:
The default name of the macro power-grid library is macros_< cellname > .cl . 
 
 Inside macro Early power-grid views: 
 
 Power net parasitic is extracted for power-pins only 
 Accurate coupling capacitance between power and ground pins is calculated from spice simulation. 
 "Current Taps" are added to the power-pins 
 No visibility of current distribution and power-grid inside macro cell. 
 
 Inside macro IR power-grid view: 
 
 Reduced distributed RC for power net and intrinsic capacitance 
 Current Taps added to merged devices in the region with relatively small current consumption (e.g. bit cells region in the memory) 
 Complete visibility inside the cell for power-grid and transistor 
 
 Inside macro EM power-grid view: 
 
 Distributed RC for power net and intrinsic capacitance 
 Current taps for each device’s contact in the design 
 Complete visibility inside the cell for power-grid and transistor

A report for the cell's power/ground net containing: 
 
 name of power and ground nets, voltage value for power pins 
 capacitance value which is simulation based 
 number of taps for the Early/EM/IR views
report containing capacitance, the current of analyzed nets, resistors, and the number of current taps and layers for the Early/EM/IR views.

title: Characterizing Current Waveforms for Macro EM Views

lines: Voltus supports characterization of the dynamic current behavior of custom macros. These current waveforms will be used during rail analysis instead of the default triangular waveform. To characterize current inside a macro PGV, the flow needs one of the following inputs:

- FSDB with Spice current waveforms
- Datasheet parameters
- User-defined PWL
- Simulation vectors
- Liberty-based vectors
- User-specified Spice deck

A user-specified trigger file is used to add the signature of current for different mode conditions. The trigger file contains information on the input vectors for which it will toggle, waveform triggering input pin information, and current information. The trigger condition is used to identify the functional mode (read/write/idle) of the custom macro in the Power and Rail Analysis flow. During power analysis, if Macro PGVs with dynamic current is provided, the software will schedule the switching events for the macro blocks based on the activity ratio. The scheduling time will be based on the timing information on the trigger pin, and all the switching events will be stored into the trigger files under the power analysis output directory. Rail analysis will load the trigger files automatically when the current files (*.pti*) are loaded. The trigger file enables you to get the current signature of the memory for different current inputs during PGV generation.

To run this flow, you should also provide a spice netlist with x,y coordinates, and Library Characterization calculates average currents through each of them and then hooks up the currents to the nearest node on the contact layer preserving the current distribution. Extraction is done till the lowest contact layer or cut layer as defined by users.

data:
When you specify a hierarchical netlist, the cell instances should have their coordinate information in the $T format. The $T format is given below: 
 
 $T=x,y,r,a 
 
 x - X-coordinate 
 
 y - Y-coordinate 
 
 r - reflection along x axis (0 or 1), 0 - No reflection, 1 - Reflection is there along x-axis 
 
 a - rotation angle in degrees (0, 90, 180, 270)

lines: The following diagram illustrates the current characterization flow:
Tcl command:
The following is the sample content of the trigger.txt file:
CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DOTLIB DOTLIB_LIST test.lib MODE_COUNT 3 SIM_STOP_TIME 10ns SIM_STEP_SIZE 20ps
For the current characterization flow, you must specify the -current_distribution { dynamic_simulation trigger_file } parameter.
The format of the trigger file is:

data:
SIMULATION - Vector-based flow 
 DOTLIB - Liberty based Flow 
 PWL - User-defined PWL flow 
 FSDB - Spice total current output captured in the FSDB format 
 DATASHEET - Datasheet details of any memory 
 SPICE_DECK_BASED - User-specified Spice deck flow

Specifies the leakage current for both power and ground pins, or just power pins. Dynamic Power Analysis will process LEAKAGE_CURRENT specified in the PGV trigger file, and use it when a macro cell is idle or dynamic switch pattern is not defined (defined as “-” ). 
 
 In the following example, the second cycle will use LEAKAGE_CURRENT defined in the PGV trigger file: 
 
 set_power -cell x -dynamic_switch_pattern { READ – READ } 
 
 You must specify the leakage current for all power pins. If leakage current is not defined for some pins, then those pins will get 0 leakage current when processed by Power Analysis. 
 
 LEAKAGE_CURRENT should be specified in the trigger file as a global, and specified outside the mode specification, as shown below: 
 
 CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD PWL CELL cell1 
 
 LEAKAGE_CURRENT { VSS 2p VDD 17n } MODE_NAME READ static ... 
 
 END_MODE END
MODE_NAME | MODE <mode_name> dynamic | static CONDITIONAL_INPUT <conditional input statement for cellname1>

Mode name and conditional input statement for the cell. You can specify either the MODE or the MODE_NAME keyword. 
 
 The optional static keyword with the MODE keyword indicates static current behavior and the dynamic keyword with the MODE keyword indicates dynamic current behavior. 
 
 Conditional input of current for the given mode.
Conditional pin statement for the cell. Signal pin for which the tool should rise or fall.

Specifies the reference time for conditional pins with respect to the specified waveform. The reference time information from the PGV is used later by Power Analysis to include the reference time with the actual arrival time in the trigger file generated by the Power Analysis engine, and by Rail Analysis to apply the reference time to the switching window. The Rail Analysis engine will use the value of reference time in the Power Analysis trigger file to calculate the waveform start time, that is equal to power_analysis_trigger_time – (ref_time) . 
 
 time can be specified as a positive or negative value.
Simulation vector detail in the given mode.

Specifies different bias conditions for capacitance characterization. This keyword allows you to simulate a macro's coupled capacitance for different modes with different bias conditions. You must specify this keyword for every mode even if the bias conditions are the same.
A label or tag to indicate a sub-mode.
The voltage values at which simulation is done for each net.

The load capacitance value for the specified sub-mode ( CUSTOM_LABEL ). 
 
 
Using the VOLTAGE and CAPACITANCE keywords, you can specify varying capacitance and voltage values for different sub-modes ( CUSTOM_LABEL ) in the PWL and FSDB-based current characterization flows. The Power Analysis engine uses the different capacitance PWLs for each mode, and scales the current based on the actual loading of the instance in the design. Rail Analysis uses the different voltage PWLs to derate the current waveform based on the voltage drop in the design. For each mode's capacitance and voltage variation, you need to specify the FSDB file ( FSDB_FILE or FSDB_PATH ) in the FSDB current characterization method, and specify the PWL file ( USER_PWL_FILE ) in the PWL current characterization method.
User-specified PWL file that contains information about the PWL waveforms (power/ground currents). The current for ground net should be specified as a negative value.
Scaling factor for peak current. This keyword is specified inside a custom label in the PWL and FSDB-based current characterization flows.
Scaling factor for time. This keyword is specified inside a custom label in the PWL and FSDB-based current characterization flows.

Specifies the net name. This keyword allows you to specify a different time and current scale factor for each power/ground net inside a custom label in the PWL and FSDB-based current characterization flows. If a net name is not specified, the same scale factor will be applied to all nets.

Specifies the time windows for different modes/sub-modes. You can specify multiple Tmin/Tmax windows for the same PWL waveform. Thereafter, the Rail Analysis engine reads this switch-bit information from the PGV, and reports the elapsed and worst IVD when the switch-bit is 1. 
 
 You can also specify negative switch-bit time. The Rail Analysis engine reads this negative switch-bit time from the PGV, and applies it from the waveform start time that was derived from the trigger file generated by the Power Analysis engine. Effective Instance Voltage (EIV) will be calculated in the switch-bit window.

DATASHEET_INPUT_SLEW <time> DATASHEET_OUTPUT_SLEW <time> DATASHEET_DELAY <time> DATASHEET_START_TIME <time> DATASHEET_PARAMETER < {power_pin cap leakage_pwr} > PEAK_CURRENT {{ power_pin1 value1} { power_pin2 value2}...}

DATASHEET_INPUT_SLEW - Clock input slew of the conditional pin 
 DATASHEET_OUTPUT_SLEW - Rising slew of the output pin Q 
 DATASHEET_DELAY - Mode delay as the read_access and write_access time 
 DATASHEET_START_TIME - Specifies the start time of the waveform 
 DATASHEET_PARAMETER - Datasheet parameters include power pin voltage, load capacitance, and leakage power. Load capacitance is mode dependent. 
 PEAK_CURRENT - Specifies peak current for the power pins. 
 When this parameter is specified, Library Generation constructs a triangular-shaped waveform with peak current matching the user-specified peak. If the area of the triangular waveform is less than the specified charge, another smaller peak is constructed before the main peak. 
 When this parameter is not specified, Library Generation constructs a trapezoid-shaped waveform whose area matches the given charge.
FSDB_PATH <path> FSDB_NET_NAME <net> [<generic_name>] FSDB_START_TIME <time> FSDB_END_TIME <time> FSDB_HIER_CHAR <hier_char, for hier DB>

FSDB file path 
 Power net name and its FSDB probe name 
 FSDB start and end time 
 Hierarchical separator for hierarchical database. The default separator is "." 
 
 Single FSDB file having total current for ALL power/ground pins with all mode current in it

Time ranges for different modes in the PWL-based current characterization flow. These keywords allow you to use a single PWL input file for multiple modes and specify different time ranges for these modes in which the dynamic PGV is being created.

Specify a single deck file for all modes, or a unique deck file for each mode. 
 User-specified Spectre simulator options. The default value of this keyword is +aps . 
 Voltus executes the Spice deck and reads the current waveform generated by simulation. After simulation of the deck file, an FSDB file is generated. You must specify the FSDB file name with the FSDB_FILE keyword.

SIMULATOR <type of simulator> DOTLIB_LIST {a list of memory dotlibs files} MODE_COUNT MODE_CONFIG_FILE <file_path> SIM_START_TIME <time> SIM_STOP_TIME <time> SIM_STEP_SIZE <step_size> STIMULUS_FILE <file_path> INCLUDE_FILE <file_path>

SIMULATOR - Specifies the simulator to be used for characterizing current waveforms for Macro EM views. You can specify the following simulators: Accelerated Parallel Simulator (APS), SPECTRE, and UltraSim. APS is the default simulator. 
 DOTLIB_LIST - Specifies a list of memory dotlibs. 
 MODE_COUNT - Specifies the maximum number of modes to be generated. Valid values are either a positive number or ` all '. ` all ' means stimulus for all power groups are to be generated. The default value is 4 . If you set this parameter to all, it may result in high runtime as it may generate too many modes. 
 MODE_CONFIG_FILE - Specifies the file containing extra information for memory cell. This file contains the following information - signal slew, signal active value, and memory write information. 
 SIM_START_TIME - Specifies the simulation start time. The default unit is in nanoseconds (ns). 
 SIM_STOP_TIME - Specifies the simulation stop time. The default unit is in nanoseconds (ns).

SIM_STEP_SIZE - Specifies the step size for power-grid analysis. The default value of step size is 20 picoseconds (ps). This parameter supports other units such as, ns and fs. This parameter supports unit-prefixes (pico, femto, nano) combined with units of measure (ps, fs, ns), but does not support units itself ("second", "Ohm", "Farad") without unit-prefix. 
 STIMULUS_FILE - Specifies the stimulus file containing the trigger information for all input ports. 
 INCLUDE_FILE - Specifies the name of the simulation include file.

lines: Trigger File Format - for simulation vector-based files
The file format of the trigger file is:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD SIMULATION SIMULATOR APS CELL <cellname1> MODE_NAME <name1> CONDITIONAL_INPUT <conditional input statement for cellname1> CONDITIONAL_PIN <pin_name> (rise|fall|both) CONDITIONAL_STIMULUS_FILE <usim1.txt> END_MODE END
where,
"!" denotes low state of the signal
"&" denotes boolean AND operation
"|" denotes boolean OR operation

For each mode, you need to specify separate stimulus files (for example usim1.txt and usim2.txt). The stimulus files contain the PWL definitions that will be used by UltraSim for simulating the specific mode.
Example 1 - Simulation

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD SIMULATION SIMULATOR APS CELL mem1 MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = (! ATPGM & EZ &! SCAN &! TEZ & TM &! TPAGE &! TWTZ &! TWZ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK {RISE} CONDITIONAL_STIMULUS_FILE usim.txt END_MODE END
Trigger File Format - for user-defined PWLs
The file format of the trigger file is:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD PWL CELL <cellname1> MODE_NAME <name1> CONDITIONAL_INPUT <conditional input statement for cellname1> CONDITIONAL_PIN <pin_name> (rise|fall|both) USER_PWL_FILE pwl.txt PWL_START_TIME <time> PWL_END_TIME <time> END_MODE END
The two formats of the PWL file are:
Format 1: Single PWL for Each Net

UNIT CURRENT <current unit> (default is A, Supported values are A, mA, uA & nA) UNIT TIME <time unit> (default is s, Supported values are s, ms, us, ns, ps) NET <net_name1> REGION {t1 i1 t2 i2 t3 i3.. } | PWLFILE net1_pwl.txt NET <net_name2> REGION {t4 i4 t5 i5 t6 i6.. } | PWLFILE net2_pwl.txt

Here, the time and current units are as defined using UNIT CURRENT and UNIT TIME syntax. NET specifies that the REGION command following it applies to the specified net name. This stays in effect until another NET or CELL specification is encountered. REGION specifies the power up piecewise linear (PWL) stimulus in (time, current) pair format. Instead of the REGION keyword, you can use the PWLFILE keyword to include a supply net (power or ground) or a switched-net-specific PWL file.
The following is an example of the file specified with the PWLFILE keyword: 0, 3 1.0e-9, 5 1.2e-9, 3 ...
Here, each line contains the time and current pairs.

Both the REGION and PWLFILE keywords can be used, but the PWLFILE keyword is recommended for long waveforms so that you can easily edit the PWL values, insert some new points, and comment out certain points that are to be ignored.
Format 2: Multiple PWLs for Each Net (Specify Multiple PWLs for Different Regions)
UNIT CURRENT mA UNIT TIME ns NET VDD REGION { 0 1.0 0.100 1.0 ... } RECT {llx1 lly1 urx1 ury1} REGION { 0 0.5 0.100 2.5 ... } RECT {llx2 lly2 urx2 ury2}...
NET VSS REGION { 0 -1.0 0.100 -1.0 ... } RECT {llx1 lly1 urx1 ury1} REGION { 0 -0.5 0.100 -2.5 ... } RECT {llx2 lly2 urx2 ury2}

This format allows you to assign multiple PWLs to different regions inside a macro. This format has multiple occurrences of the REGION keyword in the NET section. Each occurrence of the REGION keyword specifies the entire PWL waveform in the time-current pair format as a single line. This is followed by the RECT keyword that specifies the coordinates of an area inside the macro. This PWL file format allows you to model the dynamic current waveform for the taps in different areas accurately.
Example 1: User-defined PWL

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD PWL CELL mem1 MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !ATPGM & EZ & !SCAN & !TEZ & TM & !TPAGE & !TWTZ & !TWZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } USER_PWL_FILE pwl.txt END_MODE END
The following is the sample content of the pwl.txt file:
The following is the sample content of the pwl.txt file that specifies multiple PWLs for different regions:
Example 2: User-defined PWL
Following is an example of the trigger file specifying time ranges for different modes using a single PWL input file:
CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD PWL CELL MEM1
MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT !WE CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } USER_PWL_FILE pwl.txt PWL_START_TIME 0ns PWL_END_TIME 5ns END_MODE
MODE_NAME WRITE CONDITIONAL_INPUT WE CONDITIONAL_PIN { RISE } USER_PWL_FILE pwl.txt PWL_START_TIME 5ns PWL_END_TIME 10ns END_MODE END
Example 3: User-defined PWL
Following is an example of the trigger file specifying different sub-modes (custom-labels):
CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD PWL CELL MEM1
MODE_NAME READ dynamic CONDITIONAL_INPUT = !CEN CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE }
CUSTOM_LABEL A_1 VOLTAGE { VDD 0.9 VDD_LP 0.9 VSS 0 } USER_PWL_FILE pwl1.txt
CUSTOM_LABEL A_2 VOLTAGE { VDD 0.7 VDD_LP 0.7 VSS 0 } USER_PWL_FILE pwl1.txt
END_MODE ... END
Example 4: User-defined PWL
Following is an example for specifying time and current scale factor for each net in the trigger file:
CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD PWL CELL cell2 LEAKAGE_CURRENT { VSS 2p VDD 17n }
MODE_NAME READ static CONDITIONAL_INPUT = !CEN CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } CONDITIONAL_PIN_REFERENCE_TIME 0.0

CUSTOM_LABEL A1 VOLTAGE { VDD 0.9 VDD_LP 0.9 VSS 0 } CAPACITANCE 10 USER_PWL_FILE /../pwl_1.txt TIME_FACTOR=2 CURRENT_FACTOR=2 NET=VDD TIME_FACTOR=1.400 CURRENT_FACTOR=1.200 NET=VSS STATIC_INTERNAL_POWER { VSS 1p VDD 17n VDD_LP 1 } CELL_DECAP_FILE cell_decap_read.txt SWITCHBIT_WINDOW {-3 4} VSS SWITCHBIT_WINDOW {4 8} VDD
END_MODE
END
Trigger File Format - FSDB
The file format of the trigger file is:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD FSDB CELL <cellname1> MODE_NAME <name1> CONDITIONAL_INPUT <conditional input statement for cellname1> CONDITIONAL_PIN <pin_name> (rise|fall|both) FDSB_START_TIME <time> FSDB_END_TIME <time> END_MODE FSDB_PATH <path> FSDB_NET_NAME <net> [<generic_name>] END
Example 1: FSDB

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD FSDB CELL mem1 MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !ATPGM & EZ & !SCAN & !TEZ & TM & !TPAGE & !TWTZ & !TWZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } FDSB_START_TIME 0ns FSDB_END_TIME 10ns END_MODE FSDB_PATH “new.fsdb" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddpr)" "VDDPR" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddar)" "VDDAR" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddp)" "VDDP" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvss)" "VSS" END
Example 2: FSDB

You can read multiple FSDBs for different modes, and specify different start and end times for each mode. The following example shows that 2 FSDB files are read, wherein read.fsdb is read for the READ mode, and write.fsdb is read for the WRITE mode:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD FSDB CELL BLULP00256032040 MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !ATPGM & EZ & !SCAN & !TEZ & TM & !TPAGE & !TWTZ & !TWZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } FDSB_START_TIME 0ns FSDB_END_TIME 10ns FSDB_PATH "read.fsdb" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddpr)" "VDDPR" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddar)" "VDDAR" END MODE

MODE_NAME WRITE CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !ATPGM & EZ & !SCAN & !TEZ & TM & !TPAGE & !TWTZ & !TWZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } FDSB_START_TIME 0ns FSDB_END_TIME 15ns FSDB_PATH "write.fsdb" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddpr)" "VDDPR" FSDB_NET_NAME "i1(vvddar)" "VDDAR" END MODE END
Example 3: FSDB

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD FSDB CELL cellA MODE_NAME WRITE CONDITIONAL_INPUT = !CEN CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { FALL } BIAS_VOLTAGE { A[1] 0.9 A[2] 0.9 } FSDB_START_TIME 19ns FSDB_END_TIME 33ns FSDB_PATH "./tran.fsdb" END_MODE
MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = !CEN CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } BIAS_VOLTAGE { A[1] 0.9 A[2] 0.0 } FSDB_START_TIME 10ns FSDB_END_TIME 40ns FSDB_PATH "./tran.fsdb" END_MODE
Trigger File Format - Datasheet
The file format of the trigger file is:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DATASHEET CELL <cellname1> MODE_NAME <name1> CONDITIONAL_INPUT <conditional input statement for cellname1> CONDITIONAL_PIN <pin_name> (rise|fall|both) DATASHEET_DELAY <time> DATASHEET_PARAMETER < {power_pin cap leakage_pwr} > PEAK_CURRENT {power_pin peak_current} END_MODE DATASHEET_INPUT_SLEW <time> DATASHEET_OUTPUT_SLEW <time> END
Example 1: Datasheet

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DATASHEET CELL mem1 MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( WZ & EZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } DATASHEET_DELAY 7.6913ns DATASHEET_PARAMETER {{ VDDAR 0.1715pf 2.496e-05mW }{ VDDPR 18.8985pf 3.63e-06mW }{ VBBNW 0.1780pf 2.601e-06mW }} END_MODE

MODE_NAME WRITE CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !WZ & !WTZ & EZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } DATASHEET_DELAY 8.216ns DATASHEET_PARAMETER {{ VDDAR 0.1715pf 1.355e-06mW }{ VDDPR 0.1715pf 1.81e-05mW } { VBBNW 0.1715pf 2.614e-06mW }} END_MODE

MODE_NAME STANDBY CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !EZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } DATASHEET_DELAY 0.6913ns DATASHEET_PARAMETER {{ VDDAR 0.1715pf 2.902e-05mW }{ VDDPR 0.2024pf 1.81e-05mW } { VBBNW 0.1723pf 6.92e-07mW }} END_MODE
DATASHEET_INPUT_SLEW 0.6ns DATASHEET_OUTPUT_SLEW 0.3682ns END
Example 2: Datasheet

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DATASHEET CELL mem1 MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( WZ & EZ ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } DATASHEET_DELAY 7.6913ns DATASHEET_PARAMETER {{ VDD 0.1715pf 2.5e-05W }{ VDDPR 18.8985pf 3.63e-06W }{ VBBNW 0.1780pf 2.601e-06W }} PEAK_CURRENT {{ VDD 0.15mA} { VDDPR 0.1mA} { VBBNW 0.25mA}} END_MODE
DATASHEET_INPUT_SLEW 0.6ns DATASHEET_OUTPUT_SLEW 0.8ns END
Example 3: Datasheet

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DATASHEET CELL COMPILER_MEM MODE_NAME READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = (ME & !WE &!LS & !DS & !SD) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK {RISE} DATASHEET_DELAY 1.2ns DATASHEET_START_TIME 100e-12 DATASHEET_PARAMETER {{VDD 0.1193e-09 0.0025mW} {VDDM 0.00146e-09 0mW}} DATASHEET_PEAK_CURRENT {{VDD 68mA} {VDDM 0mA}} END_MODE

MODE_NAME WRITE CONDITIONAL_INPUT = (ME & !WE &!LS & !DS & !SD) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK {RISE} DATASHEET_DELAY 1.2ns DATASHEET_START_TIME 100e-12 DATASHEET_PARAMETER {{VDD 0.1813e-09 0.0025mW} {VDDM 0.00146e-09 0mW}} DATASHEET_PEAK_CURRENT {{VDD 82mA} {VDDM 0mA}} END_MODE
DATASHEET_INPUT_SLEW 5.508481e-03ns DATASHEET_OUTPUT-SLEW 3.693163e-03ns END
Trigger File Format - for user-specified Spice Deck
The file format of the trigger file is:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD SPICE_DECK_BASED CELL <cellname1> MODE_NAME <name1> CONDITIONAL_INPUT <conditional input statement for cellname1> CONDITIONAL_PIN <pin_name> (rise|fall|both) FDSB_START_TIME <time> FSDB_END_TIME <time> END_MODE DECK_FILE <filename> FSDB_NET_NAME <net> [<generic_name>] FSDB_FILE <filename> SIM_OPTIONS +aps END
Example 1: User-specified Spice Deck
CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD SPICE_DECK_BASED CELL cellA MODE READ CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !CEN & !WEN ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } FSDB_START_TIME 0ns FSDB_END_TIME 0.5ns END_MODE
MODE WRITE CONDITIONAL_INPUT = ( !CEN & !WEN ) CONDITIONAL_PIN CLK { RISE } FSDB_START_TIME 0.6ns FSDB_END_TIME 2ns END_MODE
DECK_FILE deck.cir FSDB_NET_NAME i1(vvdd) VDD FSDB_NET_NAME i1(vvss) VSS FSDB_FILE usimCktFile.fsdb.gz SIM_OPTIONS +aps
END
Trigger File Format - Dotlib

For dotlib-based current characterization, the trigger and stimulus files are automatically generated from an existing dotlib. This flow is applicable only for memory cells. Voltus generates stimuli for the worst power groups (rise_power, fall_power or power) in the specified dotlib. It reads only the internal power groups having the "when" condition and constructs stimulus vectors. Each of the generated stimulus belongs to one of the following groups:

- pin power based (this group is associated with input pin)
- arc-based (this group is associated with output pin)
The file format of the trigger file is:

CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DOTLIB DOTLIB_LIST {a list of memory dotlibs files} MODE_COUNT <specifies the maximum number of modes to be generated. Valid values are either a positive number or `all'. The default value is 4> MODE_CONFIG_FILE <file_path> SIM_STOP_TIME <time> SIM_STEP_SIZE <step_size>

If the dotlib contains arc-based power groups, you must specify MODE_CONFIG_FILE and include the memory_write condition in the memory description file. Otherwise, arc-based power groups will be skipped. The memory description file contains the following information:

- Signals slew : specify slew for signals. These slew values will be used in case rise or fall is to be applied on signal. The slew value specified using this construct takes priority over the slew value saved for the group. The default slew unit in the slews construct is ns. The syntax is: 
 slews {
<signal_1> <slew_value_1>
<signal_2> <slew_value_2>
....
}
- Signals : specify signals' active values irrespective of memory_write . The syntax is: 
 signals {
<sig_1_name> <active_value> 
<sig_2_name> <active_value> 
....
 }
 where <active value> can be H or L
- Memory write information : specify information required to write data to memory through memory_write block. 
 memory_write {
clock <clock_name> <type>, where <type> can be R, F, H or L
data_in <data_bus_name>
address <address bus name>
signal <sig_1 name> <active_value>, where <active value> can be H or L
signal <sig_2 name> <active_value>, where <active value> can be H or L
 ....
}
A sample memory description file is given below:
cell memory1 { memory_write { address A data_in D clock CLK R signal WEN L signal CEN L } slews
Example 1: Dotlib
CURRENT_CHARACTERIZATION_METHOD DOTLIB DOTLIB_LIST {} MODE_COUNT all SIM_STOP_TIME 10ns SIM_STEP_SIZE 100ps
Sample Library Generation Output Report
The following is a report generated from a power-grid library using a dotlib-based trigger file:
